slides.lead: Demo presentation of the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event name, a one-line subtitle and the meta row at the bottom here. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: The state of our channels, our data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.h1: The branch is no longer the front door.
slides.lead: Customers open accounts on a phone at midnight. Value comes from meeting them where they already are.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: more accounts opened end to end in the mobile app
average time saved per onboarding after automation
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: what moves the customer counts.
Start where you already have data and where the pain is sharpest.
A small pilot with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a caption, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The flow does not wait at a counter, it follows the customer.
bullets: Identity and documents straight from the phone camera.
Against trusted sources in seconds, not days.
Risk and eligibility before the first reply.
The account is ready to use the same evening.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same customer, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: Everything runs through a teller. Personal but slow and costly.
Appointments and callbacks. Better, yet still tied to hours.
Self-service in the app, help on demand. Lowest cost to serve.
High-value advice in branch, routine tasks in the app.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for a comparison or a list of options.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of modern retail banking.
cards: Open an account from a phone in minutes, not visits.
Clear views of where the money goes each month.
Flags unusual transactions before the customer notices.
Instant transfers and reminders that prevent late fees.
Customer data stays inside your controlled network.
A team that understands and trusts the tools.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to finish the basics well.
Digital channels improve fast and reshape habits.
Automation starts to design offers, not just process them.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: Trust is not won at the counter anymore, it is won in the seconds it takes the app to respond.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every feature has the name of a person who is accountable.
You know in advance how you will recognize success.
For high-risk steps a person always makes the call.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A pilot with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch into the app and run a full onboarding live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
